channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Court orders Biden admin to stop selling border wall materials, was 'illegally subverting' laws: Texas AG
item.description: The Biden administration has been ordered to stop selling off border materials ahead of the incoming Trump administration next month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden White House to send $1.25 billion in weapon aid to Ukraine before Trump transition: report
item.description: The Biden White House is set to announce an additional $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine as the incoming Trump administration to take office in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says her agency will need to start taking “extraordinary measures,” or special accounting maneuvers intended to prevent the nation from hitting the debt ceiling, as early as January 14th, in a letter sent to congressional leaders Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police searching for man who allegedly groped 5-year-old near New York migrant shelter
item.description: The New York Police Department Special Victims Unit is investigating after a man allegedly groped a 5-year-old girl near a government-funded migrant shelter in Midtown Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Requiring federal workers to return to office will be hard: Comer
item.description: U.S. Rep. James Comer believes evaluating the federal workforce's efficiency is critical to eliminating federal waste.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Kay Granger revelation punctuates increasing scrutiny of aging officials
item.description: The revelation that Rep. Kay Granger (R-Texas) has been living in an assisted living facility is fueling scrutiny of aging public officials, highlighting the shifting norms surrounding lawmaker health, seniority, and for how long it is appropriate to hang on to power.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Russian government says it is willing to improve ties
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg told Fox News that both Russia and Ukraine are ready for the war to end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WV Gov. Jim Justice to delay US Senate swearing-in, averting brief bedlam in Charleston
item.description: West Virginia would have had four governors in ten days if Justice were to take his Senate seat on-time, so the governor announced he will serve out his term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former FBI and CIA chief urges senators to sink Patel, Gabbard
item.description: William H. Webster, former FBI and CIA director, wrote a letter to U.S. senators urging them to reject President-elect Trump's choice of Kash Patel and Tulsi Gabbard to lead the FBI and national intelligence, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McHenry jokes sister sent him 'the cruelest things' about him on the internet after famous gavel slam
item.description: Rep. Patrick McHenry (R-N.C.) joked that his sister sent him “the cruelest things” people said about him on the internet after his famous gavel slam affirming the ouster of former Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) became a meme, the News and Observer reported.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death, poll finds
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger.
item.dc:creator: Linley Sanders, Tom Murphy And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hochul signs bill that will charge oil and gas firms $75B, but critics say customers will really foot the tab
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul signed legislation that will issue assessments to energy firms operating in New York; funds will be used for climate mitigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers push to expand agencies' power to cope with drones
item.description: Lawmakers continue to discuss legislation that would expand the authority of local police agencies to deal with drones.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: ICE deports former high-ranking Somalian military official accused of torture and terror
item.description: ICE officials in Washington, D.C., deported a former high-ranking Somalian military officer who allegedly carried out torture, terror and other human rights abuses on civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tells 37 people with sentences commuted by Biden to 'go to hell' in lengthy Christmas post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday used a lengthy Christmas Day social media post to tell the 37 people who had their death sentences commuted by President Biden to "go to hell.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to pursue executions after Biden commutes most of federal death row
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump promised on Tuesday to “vigorously pursue” capital punishment after President Joe Biden commuted the sentences of most people on federal death row partly to stop Trump from pushing forward their executions.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton is out of the hospital after being treated for the flu
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton was discharged from a Washington hospital on Tuesday and will be home for Christmas after he was admitted the day before with a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to MedStar Georgetown University Hospital in Washington after developing a fever. The 78-year-old was hospitalized in the “afternoon for testing and observation," Angel Urena, Clinton's deputy chief of staff, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A history of the Panama Canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Teddy Roosevelt once declared the Panama Canal “one of the feats to which the people of this republic will look back with the highest pride.” More than a century later, Donald Trump is threatening to take back the waterway for the same republic.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, JUAN ZAMORANO and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC
